seo.pageTitle: Jeju Eastern UNESCO Spots Small-Group Day Tour | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: 9-hour East Jeju small-group loop: Hamdeok Beach, Seongeup Folk Village (UNESCO folk heritage), two World Natural Heritage sites in Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO) and Manjanggul (UNESCO), plus a haenyeo diving performance. All admissions included.
seo.title: Jeju East UNESCO Day Tour · All Admissions Included | AtoC
seo.description: 9-hour East Jeju small-group day tour: Hamdeok Beach → Seongeup Folk Village → lunch → Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO) → haenyeo diving performance → Manjanggul (UNESCO). All admissions included, certified guide.

catalog_card.title: Jeju East UNESCO Day Tour
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: Loop eastern Jeju's UNESCO sights in a 9-hour small group. Includes Hamdeok Seoubong Beach, Seongeup Folk Village (UNESCO folk heritage), Seongsan Ilchulbong and Manjanggul — two UNESCO World Natural Heritage sites inscribed together in 2007 — plus a haenyeo diving performance (Haenyeo Museum if canceled). All admissions included, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide.
catalog_card.subtitle: Small-group minibus with English/Chinese guide — loop Hamdeok Beach, Seongeup Folk Village, Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO), the haenyeo diving performance, and Manjanggul (UNESCO) in a 9-hour eastern route.

hero.tagline: Eastern Jeju's UNESCO and signature stops in a single small-group day, with all admissions included

itineraryStops.name: Pickup (4 points across Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description:
**Pickup is consolidated at four central Jeju City points** to minimize morning crisscrossing: **Jeju International Airport (제주공항)** for guests with same-day arrivals or hotels near the airport corridor; **Jeju City Hall (제주시청)** for guests in the Yeon-dong / Nohyeong / Old Town hotel cluster; **Hagwi-dong (하귀동)** as the westernmost pickup before heading east; and **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** as the optional eastern Jeju City pickup near Tapdong waterfront. 

Sequence is Hagwi → Airport → City Hall → Dongmun → eastward Route 1132 toward Hamdeok. The minicoach is **small-group (max ~14 pax)** with English-speaking guide. 

**Bilingual printed itinerary card** distributed at pickup, weather brief at first stop.
highlights: **4 pickup points** across Jeju City — Airport, City Hall, Hagwi, Dongmun
Sequence west→east minimizes morning coach distance
**Small-group minicoach (max 14 pax)** with English guide
Bilingual itinerary card + weather brief at first stop
Total morning transit Jeju City → Hamdeok: ~40 min
Drop-off mirrors pickup with optional Dongmun Market end-of-day stop
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Four pickup points cover Jeju City's main hotel + transport zones (airport, mid-city, old town, market district) without forcing guests across the city. Sequencing west→east lands the coach at Hamdeok ~09:50 — early enough to catch the morning low-tide beach panorama before peak visitor flow.

itineraryStops.name: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
itineraryStops.category: Top-3 Jeju Beach — Olle Trail 19 + WWII Coastal History
itineraryStops.description:
**Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (함덕 서우봉 해변)** is one of Jeju's best-known east-coast beaches, loved for its bright sand, shallow emerald water, and the small volcanic cone of **Seoubong (서우봉)** rising at the eastern end. 

The main beach and connected coves give guests an easy first look at Jeju's coastal scenery without a long walk, and the seaside promenade is suitable for families and seniors. 

In spring, the slopes around Seoubong often turn yellow with canola flowers; in clear weather, the short upper path gives a broad view over the eastern coastline. 

The area also has a historical layer: coastal caves and wartime remains around Seoubong are connected with Japan's late-colonial military presence on Jeju, adding context beyond the beach view. Hamdeok is also part of **Jeju Olle Trail Course 19**, so guests can experience a small piece of Jeju's walking culture before moving inland. 

Admission is free, and the stop works well as a relaxed photo and orientation point at the beginning of the eastern route.
highlights: **Top 3 Jeju beaches** — emerald-turquoise water + white coral sand
**500 m main beach** + 3 connected coves (~900 m total length)
**Seoubong volcanic cone** ~1-hour summit climb — canola-yellow slopes March–April
**WWII Imperial Japanese Army tunnels** in adjacent cliffs — kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Olle Trail Course 19 (조천 → 김녕)** — blue-orange ribbon trail markers
**Cafe Delmoondo** — large terraces, beachfront, signature stop
Up to **50,000 daily summer visitors** at peak; uncrowded spring + autumn
**FREE admission**; 20 km / ~40 min from Jeju International Airport
Mediterranean-like color contrast — rare on the Korean Peninsula
timeUsed: Cafe Delmoondo or beachfront photography — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The morning's first stop, opening with the sea.** Right after pickup at four points across Jeju City, the day begins at Hamdeok Seoubong Beach, about a 40-minute drive away. After lightly enjoying the emerald water, white sand, and the walking heritage of Olle Trail Course 19, the route continues inland to Seongeup and on to the eastern tip at Seongsan and Manjanggul. Because it is early in the morning, it is relatively quiet even in the summer peak season.

VisitJeju — Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
Jeju 4·3 Peace Foundation — Imperial Japanese Army tunnel records
Korea Tourism Organization — Top 3 Jeju Beaches ranking
Jocheon-eup local cultural office — Seoubong oreum trail records

smartNotes.photo: Sunset with Seoubong silhouette is the classic shot; aerial-style shots from Seoubong summit show the white sand curve
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple cafés along the beachfront; convenience stores nearby
smartNotes.tip: Less crowded than Hyeopjae or Gimnyeong beaches; the Delmoondo café area on the east side is popular for sunset

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms and shower facilities at beach

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours (beach); managed swim season July–August with lifeguards
visitBasics.walking: Easy — beach is flat sand; Seoubong hike is moderate (steps)

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village (Korean National Folklore Cultural Heritage)
itineraryStops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongeup Folk Village (성읍 민속마을)** is an inhabited Joseon-era village that preserves old Jeju stone walls, thatched-roof houses, narrow alleys, and traditional household layouts. Historically, the village served as the administrative center of Jeongui-hyeon, one of the main local districts of Joseon-era Jeju, and its heritage value is recognized as an Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site. 

Unlike a staged folk park, people still live here, so the visit must be respectful and quiet around private homes. The village is also known for its old zelkova and hackberry trees near the center, traditional Jeju architecture, and local stories about daily life in a windy volcanic island environment. 

For visitors, this stop gives a grounded cultural contrast after Hamdeok's coast: guests see how Jeju people historically built homes, protected villages with stone walls, and adapted to a harsh island climate.
highlights: **Founded 1423** by Sejong the Great — administrative center until **1914 (~500 years)**
**110 traditional structures** within original eupseong walled-town footprint
**Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea** (entire village + multiple buildings)
**Two ~600-year-old trees** at center: Saw-leaf Zelkova + Japanese Hackberry — **Natural Monuments**
Designed on **Joseon Korean feng shui (geomancy) principles** — typical county-capital plan
**'Dae Jang Geum' (MBC 2003–2004)** filming location — international K-drama hit
**Omegisul (오메기술)** — rice-based Jeju liquor brewed by single family for generations (free tasting)
Historical scars: **1826 fire** destroyed ~80 buildings; **1948 Jeju Uprising** damage
**FREE admission** — village still inhabited by ~250 families maintaining traditions
timeUsed: Parking + welcome brief by village docent — ~10 min
Walk to ancient trees + village center — ~10 min
Hanok interior visits (2–3 representative homes) — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's only inland Joseon-era heritage site and its geographic pivot point.** Located on the way from the Hamdeok coast toward Seongsan (inland Pyoseon), it turns plain driving time into a meaningful stop. The stone walls and thatched roofs of this National Folklore Cultural Heritage village and its 600-year-old old trees add a 'leisurely cultural walk' that contrasts with the afternoon's volcanic and haenyeo heritage, balancing the day so it is not just a pilgrimage of photo spots.

sources: Cultural Heritage Administration — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage designation
Korea Cultural Heritage Foundation — Seongeup Folk Village historical records
VisitJeju — Seongeup Minsok Maeul
MBC Drama Archive — Dae Jang Geum (2003–2004) filming locations
Jeju Folklore Society — Omegisul brewing tradition documentation

smartNotes.photo: Stone walls (dolhareubang grandfathers), thatched-roof houses with smoke holes, and traditional doors are iconic Jeju shots
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor information center at entrance; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Hire a free volunteer guide at the entrance for cultural context; respect that residents actually live here — don't enter homes uninvited

visitBasics.admission: Free for general village; some private homes charge for guided visits
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat dirt and stone paths through the village

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (Jeju local restaurant)
itineraryStops.category: Midday Reset — East Jeju Local Cuisine
itineraryStops.description: About a 60-minute lunch break between Seongeup Folk Village and Seongsan Ilchulbong. At restaurants around Seongsan-eup harbor you can explore eastern Jeju local fare such as black pig, abalone hot pot, and cutlassfish. Lunch is not included (about ₩15,000).
highlights: **East Jeju seafood corridor** — Seongsan-eup harbor-side strip
**Mulhoe (물회)** in summer; **galchi-jorim** winter rotation
**Heuk-doeji** year-round — Cultural Heritage Material No. 5 (2015)
**Vegetarian + halal-friendly** options on request
**10-min coach to Seongsan Ilchulbong** entrance
Group dietary requirements collected at pickup
timeUsed: Coach to Seongsan Ilchulbong entrance — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **A recharge right before the core UNESCO itinerary.** At a local restaurant on the road from Seongeup to Seongsan, you have lunch of eastern Jeju local fare such as black pig, abalone hot pot, and seasonal seafood (meal not included). It is the spot to fill up before the most active afternoon — climbing Seongsan, the haenyeo diving performance, and Manjanggul.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO Sunrise Peak)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Tuff Cone — Sunrise Peak (Triple UNESCO Designation)
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. 

The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. 

**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. 

**Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run once daily at 14:00 (1 show/day) at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. 

Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. 

**Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.
highlights: **Triple UNESCO**: World Heritage 2007-07-02 + Biosphere Reserve 2002 + Global Geopark 2010-10
**5,000 years old** — Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic eruption, magma-meets-seawater steam-explosive birth
Crater **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** (the 'stone crown')
**182 m above sea level** — 0.8 km / ~140 m gain, 60-min round trip on stone-step trail
**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** at base — 1945 kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Haenyeo demonstration once daily at 14:00 (subject to bad-weather cancellation)** at the seaside cove — UNESCO Intangible 2016
**Admission updated April 2025**: Adult ₩5,000 / Youth ₩2,500 / FREE under 7 + over 65
Spring/autumn hours **05:00–19:00** (last entry 18:20)
**Closes for first Monday of each month** (occasional rest day)
timeUsed: Ascent to summit deck (0.8 km / 140 m gain) — ~30 min
Summit views + crater rim photos — ~20 min
Optional haenyeo viewing at base (14:00 show; may be canceled in bad weather) — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The first of the day's two World Natural Heritage sites.** After lunch you climb Seongsan Ilchulbong (optional climb, about 30 min round trip) to see the tuff cone that rose from an underwater eruption about 5,000 years ago. The climb leads straight into the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea of the same area, then wraps up at the Manjanggul lava tube — part of the same 2007 inscription — completing the 'Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' narrative in a single day.

sources: UNESCO WHC inscription 1264 (Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes, 2007-07-02)
UNESCO Global Geoparks Network — Jeju (2010-10 designation)
Cultural Heritage Administration — Natural Monument designation 2000-07-19
Seongsan Ilchulbong official site (seongsan.kr) — April 2025 admission update
Jeju Haenyeo Museum — UNESCO Intangible 2016 inscription record

smartNotes.photo: Summit view of Hallasan to the west and Udo island to the north on clear days; haenyeo black-sand beach view from descending path
smartNotes.facilities: Café (Fritz Coffee 1.3km away has best exterior views of the peak); ferry to Udo Island from Seongsan Port (1.5km/15 min walk)
smartNotes.tip: All stairs but well-maintained — manageable in casual clothes; family-friendly with rest stops; sunrise visits popular but crowds gather pre-dawn

visitBasics.hours: Mar–Apr & Sep–Oct: 05:00–19:00; May–Aug: 04:30–20:00; Nov–Feb: 06:00–18:00 (last ticketing 1 hour before closing)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 5,000 KRW / Youth & Child 2,500 KRW (VisitKorea reference)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — 20–30 min steady stair climb (≈ 600 stairs); benches every few minutes for rest

itineraryStops.name: Haenyeo Diving Performance (Haenyeo Museum if the performance is canceled)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity — Culture of Jeju Haenyeo (2016), live
itineraryStops.description:
**Jeju Haenyeo (women divers) culture** is a living heritage inscribed on the **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity list in 2016**. On this tour you don't watch a museum display but **a real haenyeo diving performance staged in the sea below Seongsan Ilchulbong**. **Elderly, still-active haenyeo** who have made the sea their workplace their whole lives free-dive without oxygen gear, relying on a *tewak* (float), and you can hear for yourself the distinctive whistling sumbisori breath they release when they finish a dive. 

The performance usually runs **twice a day (around 13:30 and 15:00)**, depending on sea and weather conditions, and **viewing is free**. Haenyeo are divided into **hagun, junggun, and sanggun** by depth and diving ability, with the sanggun descending **more than 10 m on a single breath**. Fewer than **4,000 haenyeo are active today, most of them over 70**, so every scene of diving you witness in person is a record of a vanishing heritage. 

**On days when the performance is canceled due to weather or waves**, we substitute the **Jeju Haenyeo Museum** in Hado-ri, Gujwa-eup (₩1,100 for adults, about 80% indoors, three exhibition halls), continuing the same UNESCO intangible heritage in depth, indoors.
highlights: **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (2016)** as a **live diving performance**, not a museum
Watch active haenyeo actually free-dive **in the sea below Seongsan Ilchulbong**
No oxygen tank, just a single *tewak* — the sanggun dive **more than 10 m on one breath**
Hear the distinctive **sumbisori** breath at the end of each dive in person
Performance usually **twice a day (around 13:30 and 15:00)**, **free viewing**, subject to weather and sea conditions
Fewer than **4,000 active haenyeo, most over 70** — a vanishing heritage
**Haenyeo Museum substitute if the performance is canceled** (₩1,100 adult, 80% indoors, three exhibition halls)
**5 min by foot or vehicle** from Seongsan Ilchulbong — a natural link with no loss of route time
timeUsed: Transfer from Seongsan Ilchulbong to the performance site — about 5 min
Performance briefing + taking a spot — about 5 min
Watching the haenyeo diving performance — about 30 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The climax where you meet UNESCO intangible heritage as a 'live event' rather than an 'exhibit'.** Right after climbing and viewing Seongsan Ilchulbong, the route flows naturally into the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea of the same area (5-min transfer). Seeing the tuff cone's volcanic geology (World Natural Heritage), then immediately seeing the intangible heritage (2016) of the haenyeo who have lived from that sea, completes the eastern route's distinctive 'volcano + people' narrative. The performance is free and usually held in the afternoon, fitting the route well. **However, it can be canceled depending on the sea and weather**, so on such days we substitute the indoor Haenyeo Museum in Hado-ri to **guarantee the haenyeo heritage experience in any weather**.

itineraryStops.name: Manjanggul Lava Tube (UNESCO World Natural Heritage · Lava Tube)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO World Natural Heritage — Geomunoreum Lava-Tube System (inscribed 2007)
itineraryStops.description:
**Manjanggul Lava Tube (Manjanggul Lava Tube)** is a lava tube formed by **lava that erupted roughly 8,000 years ago from Geomunoreum, a parasitic volcano on the flank of Hallasan**, and in **2007 it was inscribed on the UNESCO World Natural Heritage list together with Seongsan Ilchulbong and Hallasan under the name 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes'**. Because it belongs to the **same single inscribed property** as Seongsan Ilchulbong, this tour packs **two World Natural Heritage sites** into a single day. 

With a total length of about **7.4 km** (13–15 km including connected sections), it is one of the largest lava tubes in the world, and its main passage reaches **18 m wide and 23–25 m high**. Of the three entrances created by ceiling collapse, **only about a 1 km stretch from the second entrance is open to the public**, making it an **out-and-back course of about 30–45 min** in which you return the way you came. At the end of the open section stands the **world's tallest lava column at 7.6 m high**, Manjanggul's iconic photo spot. 

The inside of the cave stays at **11–13°C year-round**, cool in summer and mild in winter, but the floor is dark and somewhat damp, so we recommend **a jacket and non-slip shoes**. It is also designated a **National Natural Monument**. Admission is **₩4,000 for adults (₩3,000 for groups) / ₩2,000 for teens and children**, hours are **09:00–18:00 (last entry 17:10)**, closed the first Wednesday of every month.
highlights: **UNESCO World Natural Heritage 2007** — inscribed under 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' alongside Seongsan Ilchulbong
**Two World Natural Heritage sites in one day** — Seongsan Ilchulbong + Manjanggul
Total length about **7.4 km**, open section about **1 km** (about 30–45 min round trip)
**World's largest lava column, 7.6 m high**, at the end of the open section
Main passage **18 m wide · 23–25 m high** — one of the world's largest lava tubes
**Geomunoreum Lava-Tube System** — formed about 8,000 years ago, a National Natural Monument
Interior **11–13°C year-round** — a summer cool-down, jacket recommended
Admission **₩4,000 adult / ₩2,000 teens and children**, hours **09:00–18:00 (last entry 17:10)**
timeUsed: Descent into the second entrance + entering the cave — about 5 min
1 km open-section round trip (to the lava column) — about 30 min
Lava-tube formation talk at the information center — about 10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's second UNESCO World Natural Heritage site and its natural finale.** After Seongsan Ilchulbong (a triple-UNESCO tuff cone) and the haenyeo diving performance (intangible heritage), Manjanggul in Gimnyeong sits naturally on the route back from Seongsan to Jeju City. Because it is part of the **same single 2007 inscription** as Seongsan Ilchulbong, pairing the two in one day completes the UNESCO 'Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' narrative. Placing it in the late afternoon **avoids the midday heat and group crowds**, and ends the day on a pleasant note in a **cool 11–13°C cave** after the outdoor itinerary. Should Manjanggul be closed for maintenance or weather, we substitute **Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land**, part of the same lava-tube system, to preserve the cave experience.

itineraryStops.name: Drop-off (2 points + Dongmun Market)
itineraryStops.description:
**Drop-off mirrors the morning pickup** with optional Dongmun Market end-of-day stop: **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** is a 25-min coach from Haenyeo Museum and offers Jeju's most popular evening market for **fresh seafood, Jeju tangerine products, and street-food dinner options** — guests can choose to drop here for independent dining + market shopping or continue to **Jeju City Hall** and **Jeju International Airport** drop-off points. 

Estimated arrival: **Dongmun Market ~17:55, City Hall ~18:10, Airport ~18:25**. Same-day evening flights from JIA (last departure ~21:30) are typically viable from this drop-off timing.
highlights: **3 drop-off points** — Dongmun Market, City Hall, Airport
**Dongmun Traditional Market** — Jeju's most popular evening market, seafood + tangerine + street food
Estimated arrival: Dongmun ~17:55, City Hall ~18:10, Airport ~18:25
**Same-day evening flights from JIA** typically viable (last departure ~21:30)
Drop-off sequence aligned with morning pickup mirroring
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Dongmun Market drop-off = optional dinner stop for guests who want to end the day with Jeju seafood + market browsing rather than coach return to hotel. City Hall + Airport drop-offs cover hotel-zone + departure-flight guests. Total post-Haenyeo Museum transit ~55 min covers all 3 points without backtracking.

routePhases.phase: From the coast to inland cultural heritage
routePhases.description: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (60 min) → Seongeup Folk Village (Joseon-era National Folklore Cultural Heritage, 60 min).
Local restaurant near Seongsan — black pig, abalone hot pot, seasonal seafood. Lunch not included.
Seongsan Ilchulbong (optional climb) → haenyeo diving performance (Haenyeo Museum if canceled) → Manjanggul lava tube (UNESCO World Natural Heritage).
Gimnyeong → transfer to Jeju City + drop-off at two points and Dongmun Market.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: An East Jeju loop that connects the island's two UNESCO World Natural Heritage sites in a single day from the eastern tip — Seongsan Ilchulbong (a tuff cone) and Manjanggul (a lava tube), both inscribed in 2007 as the same property. To these it adds the haenyeo diving performance, a UNESCO intangible heritage. An efficient route that avoids the crowds at the major sights.

Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: Two UNESCO World Natural Heritage sites + intangible heritage in one day
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong and Manjanggul are World Natural Heritage sites inscribed together in 2007 as 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes'. Add the haenyeo diving performance, a 2016 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity, and you see three layers of UNESCO heritage in a single day.

items.label: The real Manjanggul — the original UNESCO lava tube, no longer a substitute
items.detail: In the past we substituted Micheon Cave during closures, but now you explore Manjanggul itself — one of the world's largest lava tubes, home to the world's largest lava column at 7.6 m high.

items.label: All admissions included — the value at this price
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000 + Manjanggul ₩4,000 (Seongeup free, haenyeo diving performance free) — two World Natural Heritage sites are covered by just ₩9,000 in admissions.

items.label: Seongsan Ilchulbong is an optional climb
items.detail: The roughly 30-minute round-trip climb to the crater rim is steep and not right for everyone. There is also a coastal loop path with a similar view and no stairs, so you can choose according to your fitness.

content: Moderate difficulty.
Hamdeok Beach is flat sand.
Seongeup Folk Village has dirt paths and stone walls.

The haenyeo diving performance is viewed from the flat shore.
Manjanggul is about a 1 km flat round trip, with stairs at the cave entrance and a somewhat uneven, damp floor inside (11–13°C year-round, jacket recommended).
Closed-toe shoes required.

practicalAccordionItems.preview: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube included in the route.
content: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube inscribed in 2007 and is included in this route.
The open section is about a 1 km round trip (about 30–45 min), with the world's largest lava column at 7.6 m high at the end.

Strollers are not recommended on Seongsan stairs or Seongeup village paths.

staticQuestions.question: Can I actually watch a haenyeo diving performance?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. Watching the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea near Seongsan Ilchulbong is part of the route (usually in the afternoon, free). It can be canceled depending on sea and weather conditions, and if canceled we substitute the indoor Haenyeo Museum in Hado-ri to guarantee the haenyeo heritage experience.

staticQuestions.question: Can I skip the Seongsan Ilchulbong crater climb?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. There is a coastal loop path that offers a similar photo angle without stairs. The crater climb (about 0.8 km round trip, about 30 min) is optional, so you can decide according to your fitness.

staticQuestions.question: What is the difference from the southern Jeju UNESCO tour?
staticQuestions.answer: It is a tour by the same operator. The southern tour includes a Hallasan oreum, Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon Falls, the columnar joints, and the Osulloc tea fields. The eastern tour focuses on Seongsan Ilchulbong, Seongeup Folk Village, Manjanggul, and the haenyeo diving performance.

staticQuestions.question: What happens on days when Manjanggul is closed?
staticQuestions.answer: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube included in the route. However, on days it is closed for maintenance or weather (heavy rain, etc.), we substitute a nearby cave such as Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land, part of the same lava-tube system, to keep the cave experience.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: It is not included. Lunch is separate (about ₩15,000), and the guide will recommend a good local spot.

matching_metadata.unesco_fit_note: unesco_fit=5 = 2 World Heritage Sites (Seongsan Ilchulbong AND Manjanggul Lava Tube — both inscribed 2007 under 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes') + 1 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (Culture of Jeju Haenyeo, inscribed 2016, experienced live via the haenyeo diving performance with the Haenyeo Museum as the weather/sea fallback). Seongeup Folk Village is Korea's national folk heritage — NOT UNESCO — and does not contribute to this score.
matching_metadata._internal_competing_products_note: Comp set (internal only): Southern UNESCO sister product as primary peer reference.
matching_metadata.audit_summary: East Jeju UNESCO-anchored loop carrying TWO World Heritage Sites from the same 2007 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' inscription in a single day — Seongsan Ilchulbong (Surtseyan tuff cone) and Manjanggul Lava Tube (world's tallest lava column, 7.6 m) — plus UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity experienced live as a haenyeo free-diving performance beside Seongsan (Haenyeo Museum fallback when the sea show is cancelled), the Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of Seongeup Folk Village (1423 founding), and Olle Trail Course 19 walking heritage at Hamdeok. UNESCO-anchored counterpart to the signature-nature sister tour, which emphasizes Seolmundae Halmang mythology + Tadao Ando architecture instead.

high_scores.haenyeo_culture_fit=1.0: UNESCO Intangible 2016 — seen live as a haenyeo diving performance beside Seongsan, with the Haenyeo Museum as fallback

matching_metadata.differentiation_vs_signature_nature_sist: Shares the Seongsan + Seongeup anchors but swaps the sister's mythology/architecture framing for a UNESCO-heritage spine: Hamdeok Olle 19 + TWO 2007 World Heritage Sites (Seongsan tuff cone + Manjanggul lava tube) + live UNESCO Intangible haenyeo performance (2016). This tour = twin World Heritage Sites + strongest rain-resilient anchor count + best ₩-per-UNESCO-anchor value; sister = mythology + Pulitzer architecture + 4 K-drama filming sites. itineraryStops.name: Pickup (4 points across Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description:
**Pickup is consolidated at four central Jeju City points** to minimize morning crisscrossing: **Jeju International Airport (제주공항)** for guests with same-day arrivals or hotels near the airport corridor; **Jeju City Hall (제주시청)** for guests in the Yeon-dong / Nohyeong / Old Town hotel cluster; **Hagwi-dong (하귀동)** as the westernmost pickup before heading east; and **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** as the optional eastern Jeju City pickup near Tapdong waterfront. 

Sequence is Hagwi → Airport → City Hall → Dongmun → eastward Route 1132 toward Hamdeok. The minicoach is **small-group (max ~14 pax)** with English-speaking guide. 

**Bilingual printed itinerary card** distributed at pickup, weather brief at first stop.
highlights: **4 pickup points** across Jeju City — Airport, City Hall, Hagwi, Dongmun
Sequence west→east minimizes morning coach distance
**Small-group minicoach (max 14 pax)** with English guide
Bilingual itinerary card + weather brief at first stop
Total morning transit Jeju City → Hamdeok: ~40 min
Drop-off mirrors pickup with optional Dongmun Market end-of-day stop
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Four pickup points cover Jeju City's main hotel + transport zones (airport, mid-city, old town, market district) without forcing guests across the city. Sequencing west→east lands the coach at Hamdeok ~09:50 — early enough to catch the morning low-tide beach panorama before peak visitor flow.

itineraryStops.name: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
itineraryStops.category: Top-3 Jeju Beach — Olle Trail 19 + WWII Coastal History
itineraryStops.description:
**Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (함덕 서우봉 해변)** is one of Jeju's best-known east-coast beaches, loved for its bright sand, shallow emerald water, and the small volcanic cone of **Seoubong (서우봉)** rising at the eastern end. 

The main beach and connected coves give guests an easy first look at Jeju's coastal scenery without a long walk, and the seaside promenade is suitable for families and seniors. 

In spring, the slopes around Seoubong often turn yellow with canola flowers; in clear weather, the short upper path gives a broad view over the eastern coastline. 

The area also has a historical layer: coastal caves and wartime remains around Seoubong are connected with Japan's late-colonial military presence on Jeju, adding context beyond the beach view. Hamdeok is also part of **Jeju Olle Trail Course 19**, so guests can experience a small piece of Jeju's walking culture before moving inland. 

Admission is free, and the stop works well as a relaxed photo and orientation point at the beginning of the eastern route.
highlights: **Top 3 Jeju beaches** — emerald-turquoise water + white coral sand
**500 m main beach** + 3 connected coves (~900 m total length)
**Seoubong volcanic cone** ~1-hour summit climb — canola-yellow slopes March–April
**WWII Imperial Japanese Army tunnels** in adjacent cliffs — kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Olle Trail Course 19 (조천 → 김녕)** — blue-orange ribbon trail markers
**Cafe Delmoondo** — large terraces, beachfront, signature stop
Up to **50,000 daily summer visitors** at peak; uncrowded spring + autumn
**FREE admission**; 20 km / ~40 min from Jeju International Airport
Mediterranean-like color contrast — rare on the Korean Peninsula
timeUsed: Cafe Delmoondo or beachfront photography — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The morning's first stop, opening with the sea.** Right after pickup at four points across Jeju City, the day begins at Hamdeok Seoubong Beach, about a 40-minute drive away. After lightly enjoying the emerald water, white sand, and the walking heritage of Olle Trail Course 19, the route continues inland to Seongeup and on to the eastern tip at Seongsan and Manjanggul. Because it is early in the morning, it is relatively quiet even in the summer peak season.

VisitJeju — Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
Jeju 4·3 Peace Foundation — Imperial Japanese Army tunnel records
Korea Tourism Organization — Top 3 Jeju Beaches ranking
Jocheon-eup local cultural office — Seoubong oreum trail records

smartNotes.photo: Sunset with Seoubong silhouette is the classic shot; aerial-style shots from Seoubong summit show the white sand curve
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple cafés along the beachfront; convenience stores nearby
smartNotes.tip: Less crowded than Hyeopjae or Gimnyeong beaches; the Delmoondo café area on the east side is popular for sunset

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms and shower facilities at beach

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours (beach); managed swim season July–August with lifeguards
visitBasics.walking: Easy — beach is flat sand; Seoubong hike is moderate (steps)

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village (Korean National Folklore Cultural Heritage)
itineraryStops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongeup Folk Village (성읍 민속마을)** is an inhabited Joseon-era village that preserves old Jeju stone walls, thatched-roof houses, narrow alleys, and traditional household layouts. Historically, the village served as the administrative center of Jeongui-hyeon, one of the main local districts of Joseon-era Jeju, and its heritage value is recognized as an Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site. 

Unlike a staged folk park, people still live here, so the visit must be respectful and quiet around private homes. The village is also known for its old zelkova and hackberry trees near the center, traditional Jeju architecture, and local stories about daily life in a windy volcanic island environment. 

For visitors, this stop gives a grounded cultural contrast after Hamdeok's coast: guests see how Jeju people historically built homes, protected villages with stone walls, and adapted to a harsh island climate.
highlights: **Founded 1423** by Sejong the Great — administrative center until **1914 (~500 years)**
**110 traditional structures** within original eupseong walled-town footprint
**Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea** (entire village + multiple buildings)
**Two ~600-year-old trees** at center: Saw-leaf Zelkova + Japanese Hackberry — **Natural Monuments**
Designed on **Joseon Korean feng shui (geomancy) principles** — typical county-capital plan
**'Dae Jang Geum' (MBC 2003–2004)** filming location — international K-drama hit
**Omegisul (오메기술)** — rice-based Jeju liquor brewed by single family for generations (free tasting)
Historical scars: **1826 fire** destroyed ~80 buildings; **1948 Jeju Uprising** damage
**FREE admission** — village still inhabited by ~250 families maintaining traditions
timeUsed: Parking + welcome brief by village docent — ~10 min
Walk to ancient trees + village center — ~10 min
Hanok interior visits (2–3 representative homes) — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's only inland Joseon-era heritage site and its geographic pivot point.** Located on the way from the Hamdeok coast toward Seongsan (inland Pyoseon), it turns plain driving time into a meaningful stop. The stone walls and thatched roofs of this National Folklore Cultural Heritage village and its 600-year-old old trees add a 'leisurely cultural walk' that contrasts with the afternoon's volcanic and haenyeo heritage, balancing the day so it is not just a pilgrimage of photo spots.

smartNotes.facilities: Visitor information center at entrance; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Hire a free volunteer guide at the entrance for cultural context; respect that residents actually live here — don't enter homes uninvited

**Vegetarian + halal-friendly** options on request

It is the spot to fill up before the most active afternoon — climbing Seongsan, the haenyeo diving performance, and Manjanggul.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO Sunrise Peak)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Tuff Cone — Sunrise Peak (Triple UNESCO Designation)
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. 

The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. 

**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. 

**Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run once daily at 14:00 (1 show/day) at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. 

Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. 

**Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.
highlights: **Triple UNESCO**: World Heritage 2007-07-02 + Biosphere Reserve 2002 + Global Geopark 2010-10
**5,000 years old** — Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic eruption, magma-meets-seawater steam-explosive birth
Crater **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** (the 'stone crown')
**182 m above sea level** — 0.8 km / ~140 m gain, 60-min round trip on stone-step trail
**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** at base — 1945 kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Haenyeo demonstration once daily at 14:00 (subject to bad-weather cancellation)** at the seaside cove — UNESCO Intangible 2016
**Admission updated April 2025**: Adult ₩5,000 / Youth ₩2,500 / FREE under 7 + over 65
Spring/autumn hours **05:00–19:00** (last entry 18:20)
**Closes for first Monday of each month** (occasional rest day)
timeUsed: Ascent to summit deck (0.8 km / 140 m gain) — ~30 min
Summit views + crater rim photos — ~20 min
Optional haenyeo viewing at base (14:00 show; may be canceled in bad weather) — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The first of the day's two World Natural Heritage sites.** After lunch you climb Seongsan Ilchulbong (optional climb, about 30 min round trip) to see the tuff cone that rose from an underwater eruption about 5,000 years ago. The climb leads straight into the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea of the same area, then wraps up at the Manjanggul lava tube — part of the same 2007 inscription — completing the 'Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' narrative in a single day.

smartNotes.facilities: Café (Fritz Coffee 1.3km away has best exterior views of the peak); ferry to Udo Island from Seongsan Port (1.5km/15 min walk)

itineraryStops.name: Haenyeo Diving Performance (Haenyeo Museum if the performance is canceled)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity — Culture of Jeju Haenyeo (2016), live
itineraryStops.description:
**Jeju Haenyeo (women divers) culture** is a living heritage inscribed on the **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity list in 2016**. On this tour you don't watch a museum display but **a real haenyeo diving performance staged in the sea below Seongsan Ilchulbong**. **Elderly, still-active haenyeo** who have made the sea their workplace their whole lives free-dive without oxygen gear, relying on a *tewak* (float), and you can hear for yourself the distinctive whistling sumbisori breath they release when they finish a dive. 

The performance usually runs **twice a day (around 13:30 and 15:00)**, depending on sea and weather conditions, and **viewing is free**. Haenyeo are divided into **hagun, junggun, and sanggun** by depth and diving ability, with the sanggun descending **more than 10 m on a single breath**. Fewer than **4,000 haenyeo are active today, most of them over 70**, so every scene of diving you witness in person is a record of a vanishing heritage. 

**On days when the performance is canceled due to weather or waves**, we substitute the **Jeju Haenyeo Museum** in Hado-ri, Gujwa-eup (₩1,100 for adults, about 80% indoors, three exhibition halls), continuing the same UNESCO intangible heritage in depth, indoors.
highlights: **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (2016)** as a **live diving performance**, not a museum
Watch active haenyeo actually free-dive **in the sea below Seongsan Ilchulbong**
No oxygen tank, just a single *tewak* — the sanggun dive **more than 10 m on one breath**
Hear the distinctive **sumbisori** breath at the end of each dive in person
Performance usually **twice a day (around 13:30 and 15:00)**, **free viewing**, subject to weather and sea conditions
Fewer than **4,000 active haenyeo, most over 70** — a vanishing heritage
**Haenyeo Museum substitute if the performance is canceled** (₩1,100 adult, 80% indoors, three exhibition halls)
**5 min by foot or vehicle** from Seongsan Ilchulbong — a natural link with no loss of route time
timeUsed: Transfer from Seongsan Ilchulbong to the performance site — about 5 min
Performance briefing + taking a spot — about 5 min
Watching the haenyeo diving performance — about 30 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The climax where you meet UNESCO intangible heritage as a 'live event' rather than an 'exhibit'.** Right after climbing and viewing Seongsan Ilchulbong, the route flows naturally into the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea of the same area (5-min transfer). Seeing the tuff cone's volcanic geology (World Natural Heritage), then immediately seeing the intangible heritage (2016) of the haenyeo who have lived from that sea, completes the eastern route's distinctive 'volcano + people' narrative. The performance is free and usually held in the afternoon, fitting the route well. **However, it can be canceled depending on the sea and weather**, so on such days we substitute the indoor Haenyeo Museum in Hado-ri to **guarantee the haenyeo heritage experience in any weather**.

sources: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage — Culture of Jeju Haenyeo (2016 inscription)
Jeju Haenyeo Museum official site (haenyeo.go.kr)
VisitJeju — Seongsan Haenyeo diving performance
Jeju Special Self-Governing Province — haenyeo population census records

itineraryStops.name: Manjanggul Lava Tube (UNESCO World Natural Heritage · Lava Tube)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO World Natural Heritage — Geomunoreum Lava-Tube System (inscribed 2007)
itineraryStops.description:
**Manjanggul Lava Tube (Manjanggul Lava Tube)** is a lava tube formed by **lava that erupted roughly 8,000 years ago from Geomunoreum, a parasitic volcano on the flank of Hallasan**, and in **2007 it was inscribed on the UNESCO World Natural Heritage list together with Seongsan Ilchulbong and Hallasan under the name 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes'**. Because it belongs to the **same single inscribed property** as Seongsan Ilchulbong, this tour packs **two World Natural Heritage sites** into a single day. 

With a total length of about **7.4 km** (13–15 km including connected sections), it is one of the largest lava tubes in the world, and its main passage reaches **18 m wide and 23–25 m high**. Of the three entrances created by ceiling collapse, **only about a 1 km stretch from the second entrance is open to the public**, making it an **out-and-back course of about 30–45 min** in which you return the way you came. At the end of the open section stands the **world's tallest lava column at 7.6 m high**, Manjanggul's iconic photo spot. 

The inside of the cave stays at **11–13°C year-round**, cool in summer and mild in winter, but the floor is dark and somewhat damp, so we recommend **a jacket and non-slip shoes**. It is also designated a **National Natural Monument**. Admission is **₩4,000 for adults (₩3,000 for groups) / ₩2,000 for teens and children**, hours are **09:00–18:00 (last entry 17:10)**, closed the first Wednesday of every month.
highlights: **UNESCO World Natural Heritage 2007** — inscribed under 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' alongside Seongsan Ilchulbong
**Two World Natural Heritage sites in one day** — Seongsan Ilchulbong + Manjanggul
Total length about **7.4 km**, open section about **1 km** (about 30–45 min round trip)
**World's largest lava column, 7.6 m high**, at the end of the open section
Main passage **18 m wide · 23–25 m high** — one of the world's largest lava tubes
**Geomunoreum Lava-Tube System** — formed about 8,000 years ago, a National Natural Monument
Interior **11–13°C year-round** — a summer cool-down, jacket recommended
Admission **₩4,000 adult / ₩2,000 teens and children**, hours **09:00–18:00 (last entry 17:10)**
timeUsed: Descent into the second entrance + entering the cave — about 5 min
1 km open-section round trip (to the lava column) — about 30 min
Lava-tube formation talk at the information center — about 10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's second UNESCO World Natural Heritage site and its natural finale.** After Seongsan Ilchulbong (a triple-UNESCO tuff cone) and the haenyeo diving performance (intangible heritage), Manjanggul in Gimnyeong sits naturally on the route back from Seongsan to Jeju City. Because it is part of the **same single 2007 inscription** as Seongsan Ilchulbong, pairing the two in one day completes the UNESCO 'Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' narrative. Placing it in the late afternoon **avoids the midday heat and group crowds**, and ends the day on a pleasant note in a **cool 11–13°C cave** after the outdoor itinerary. Should Manjanggul be closed for maintenance or weather, we substitute **Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land**, part of the same lava-tube system, to preserve the cave experience.

smartNotes.photo: The **world's largest lava column at 7.6 m high** at the end of the open section is the signature shot, and a perspective image down the long, 18 m wide, 23 m high passage is overwhelming in scale.
smartNotes.facilities: The information center beside the entrance parking lot explains how the lava tube formed — we recommend a 10-minute look before entering. There is no shop inside the cave.
smartNotes.tip: Bring a jacket even in summer (interior 11–13°C). The floor is damp and dark, so non-slip shoes are best. The cave can close for maintenance, so check whether it is operating on your visit date in advance.

itineraryStops.name: Drop-off (2 points + Dongmun Market)
itineraryStops.description:
**Drop-off mirrors the morning pickup** with optional Dongmun Market end-of-day stop: **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** is a 25-min coach from Haenyeo Museum and offers Jeju's most popular evening market for **fresh seafood, Jeju tangerine products, and street-food dinner options** — guests can choose to drop here for independent dining + market shopping or continue to **Jeju City Hall** and **Jeju International Airport** drop-off points. 

Estimated arrival: **Dongmun Market ~17:55, City Hall ~18:10, Airport ~18:25**. Same-day evening flights from JIA (last departure ~21:30) are typically viable from this drop-off timing.

routePhases.phase: From the coast to inland cultural heritage
routePhases.description: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (60 min) → Seongeup Folk Village (Joseon-era National Folklore Cultural Heritage, 60 min).
Local restaurant near Seongsan — black pig, abalone hot pot, seasonal seafood. Lunch not included.
Seongsan Ilchulbong (optional climb) → haenyeo diving performance (Haenyeo Museum if canceled) → Manjanggul lava tube (UNESCO World Natural Heritage).
Gimnyeong → transfer to Jeju City + drop-off at two points and Dongmun Market.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: An East Jeju loop that connects the island's two UNESCO World Natural Heritage sites in a single day from the eastern tip — Seongsan Ilchulbong (a tuff cone) and Manjanggul (a lava tube), both inscribed in 2007 as the same property. To these it adds the haenyeo diving performance, a UNESCO intangible heritage. An efficient route that avoids the crowds at the major sights.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: Two UNESCO World Natural Heritage sites + intangible heritage in one day
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong and Manjanggul are World Natural Heritage sites inscribed together in 2007 as 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes'. Add the haenyeo diving performance, a 2016 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity, and you see three layers of UNESCO heritage in a single day.

items.label: The real Manjanggul — the original UNESCO lava tube, no longer a substitute
items.detail: In the past we substituted Micheon Cave during closures, but now you explore Manjanggul itself — one of the world's largest lava tubes, home to the world's largest lava column at 7.6 m high.

items.label: All admissions included — the value at this price
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000 + Manjanggul ₩4,000 (Seongeup free, haenyeo diving performance free) — two World Natural Heritage sites are covered by just ₩9,000 in admissions.

items.label: Seongsan Ilchulbong is an optional climb
items.detail: The roughly 30-minute round-trip climb to the crater rim is steep and not right for everyone. There is also a coastal loop path with a similar view and no stairs, so you can choose according to your fitness.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Air-conditioned mini-coach, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide, ALL attraction…
content: Air-conditioned mini-coach, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide, ALL attraction admissions (Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000, Seongeup Folk Village free, Ilchul Land/Micheon Cave ₩12,000, Haenyeo Museum ₩1,100, Hamdeok Beach free), fuel/tolls/parking.
Not included: lunch (≈ ₩15,000), drinks, tips.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Sequential pickup at four points: Ocean Suites Jeju (08:30), Jeju Airport Gate 1, 1F…
content: Sequential pickup at four points: Ocean Suites Jeju (08:30), Jeju Airport Gate 1, 1F Domestic Arrivals (08:45 — note: different gate from operator's Southern tour), LOTTE City Hotel Jeju (08:55), Shilla Duty-Free Jeju Store (09:05).
Choose the closest at booking.
Day-before WhatsApp confirmation.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
content: Moderate difficulty.
Hamdeok Beach is flat sand.
Seongeup Folk Village has dirt paths and stone walls.
The Seongsan Ilchulbong crater-rim climb is about 30 min of steep stone steps (can be skipped).
The haenyeo diving performance is viewed from the flat shore.
Manjanggul is about a 1 km flat round trip, with stairs at the cave entrance and a somewhat uneven, damp floor inside (11–13°C year-round, jacket recommended).
Closed-toe shoes required.
Total walking about 4–5 km.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup.
content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup.
Same-day cancellations and no-shows non-refundable.
Heavy weather may shorten the Seongsan climb but does not cancel the day.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cave information (Manjanggul)
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube included in the route.
content: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube inscribed in 2007 and is included in this route.
The open section is about a 1 km round trip (about 30–45 min), with the world's largest lava column at 7.6 m high at the end.
The interior is a cool 11–13°C year-round, so a jacket is recommended.
If closed for maintenance or weather, it may be substituted with a nearby lava tube (Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land) — confirm when booking.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Recommended for ages 10+ due to the Seongsan Ilchulbong rim climb (≈ 600 stairs, optional…
content: Recommended for ages 10+ due to the Seongsan Ilchulbong rim climb (≈ 600 stairs, optional but the day's centerpiece) and a 9-hour pace.
Younger children can join, but the climb may be too much; consider our year-round Cherry Blossom or Cruise Bus tours which skip the summit.
Strollers are not recommended on Seongsan stairs or Seongeup village paths.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Eastern Jeju has the cleanest sea views on the island and the warmest summer temperatures. Seongsan Ilchulbong is exposed to wind year-round; bring a light layer.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms in early April; rapeseed flowers on Hamdeok's Seoubong cone.

Wind chill significant at Seongsan rim. Fewer crowds at all stops.

Quietest crowds; Haenyeo Museum particularly valuable in cold weather.

staticQuestions.question: Can I actually watch a haenyeo diving performance?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. Watching the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea near Seongsan Ilchulbong is part of the route (usually in the afternoon, free). It can be canceled depending on sea and weather conditions, and if canceled we substitute the indoor Haenyeo Museum in Hado-ri to guarantee the haenyeo heritage experience.

staticQuestions.question: Can I skip the Seongsan Ilchulbong crater climb?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. There is a coastal loop path that offers a similar photo angle without stairs. The crater climb (about 0.8 km round trip, about 30 min) is optional, so you can decide according to your fitness.

staticQuestions.question: What is the difference from the southern Jeju UNESCO tour?
staticQuestions.answer: It is a tour by the same operator. The southern tour includes a Hallasan oreum, Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon Falls, the columnar joints, and the Osulloc tea fields. The eastern tour focuses on Seongsan Ilchulbong, Seongeup Folk Village, Manjanggul, and the haenyeo diving performance.

staticQuestions.question: What happens on days when Manjanggul is closed?
staticQuestions.answer: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube included in the route. However, on days it is closed for maintenance or weather (heavy rain, etc.), we substitute a nearby cave such as Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land, part of the same lava-tube system, to keep the cave experience.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: It is not included. Lunch is separate (about ₩15,000), and the guide will recommend a good local spot.

_score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale. Each score grounded in verified facts.

unesco_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 1.0 — this tour is correctly named 'eastern UNESCO loop': Seongsan = UNESCO World Heritage (Jeju Volcanic Island & Lava Tubes 2007), Haenyeo Museum = UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage (haenyeo culture 2016). Tour covers BOTH UNESCO categories. Strongest UNESCO-fit tour in catalogue.

intangible_heritage_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 1.0 — Haenyeo Museum is the dedicated UNESCO ICH interpretation center; Seongsan haenyeo demonstration at 14:00 (1 show/day) is the live UNESCO ICH performance; Seongeup Folk Village preserves Joseon-period intangible folkways. 3 of 5 attractions carry intangible-heritage value. Highest possible score.

walking_level.reasoning: DOWNGRADED 0.8→0.65 with verified totals: Hamdeok beach 0.5 km flat (sand + boardwalk), Seongeup ~1 km flat olle, Micheon Cave 365 m flat (leveled), Seongsan 0.8 km + 140 m gain (the only stair-heavy stop), Haenyeo Museum 100% indoor flat. Total ~3 km + only Seongsan has elevation. Tour 1 (east-signature) walks more (Seopjikoji 2.1 km extra). Verified moderate walking = 0.65 not 0.8.

young_kids_fit.reasoning: DOWNGRADED 1.0→0.75: Hamdeok beach + leveled Micheon Cave + indoor Haenyeo Museum + Seongeup folk village = 4 of 5 kid-friendly. ONLY Seongsan's 300-500 steps challenge under-7s. Mechanical 1.0 was overstated; verified 0.75 is honest.

stroller_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.50: Hamdeok flat + Micheon Cave LEVELED FLOOR (verified — vs Manjanggul's uneven natural floor) + Haenyeo Museum 100% accessible + Seongeup main alleys + ONLY Seongsan stairs unsuitable. 4/5 stroller-friendly = 0.50 not the previous 0.4. Specifically chose Micheon vs Manjanggul for accessibility.

mobility_friendly_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.65: Verified 4 of 5 stops mobility-friendly (Hamdeok flat, Micheon leveled, Haenyeo Museum elevators, Seongeup flat alleys), only Seongsan summit not accessible. The choice of Micheon Cave instead of Manjanggul specifically supports this.

weather_sensitivity.reasoning: DOWNGRADED 1.0→0.75 — verified: Haenyeo Museum 100% indoor, Micheon Cave 100% indoor and stable temperature year-round, Seongeup hanok have shelter. Only Hamdeok and Seongsan are weather-exposed. The 'all UNESCO' name made the prior score look high, but indoor ratio is actually reasonable. Mechanical 1.0 overstated.

indoor_ratio.reasoning: MAJOR CORRECTION: Verified breakdown — Hamdeok 100% outdoor, Seongeup ~30% indoor (visiting hanok interiors), Micheon Cave 100% indoor, Seongsan 5% indoor (visitor center only), Haenyeo Museum 100% indoor. Time-weighted indoor ratio ~40%, NOT the previous 10%. Correcting a documentation error.

rain_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.75: With Micheon Cave (indoor), Haenyeo Museum (indoor), and Seongeup hanok interiors as rain refuges, this tour holds up MUCH better in rain than the mechanical 0.6 implied. Hamdeok and Seongsan suffer in rain but the rest is fine. Verified.

iconic_landmark_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 0.85: Seongsan = universally iconic (triple UNESCO crown). Hamdeok = Top 3 Jeju beach. Seongeup + Haenyeo Museum = nationally significant culture (UNESCO ICH for Haenyeo). Micheon Cave = secondary lava tube. 0.85 — strong but not all 5 universally iconic.

value_for_money_fit.reasoning: Verified fees: Hamdeok free + Seongeup free + Micheon ₩12,000 + Seongsan ₩2,000 + Haenyeo Museum ₩1,100 = ~₩15,100/adult. Reasonable but Ilchul Land at ₩12,000 is the big driver. 0.85 calibrated.

senior_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.55: With Hamdeok beach walk (flat), Micheon leveled cave, Seongeup flat alleys, Haenyeo Museum accessible — 4 of 5 stops fully senior-accessible. Only Seongsan summit excludes seniors with mobility issues (alternative: stay at base for haenyeo demo). Verified ≥0.55, much better than 0.4.

senior_active_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.75: For active seniors, all 5 stops are achievable (Seongsan rest stops + benches make 45-60 min summit reasonable). Cultural depth (UNESCO + 600-year village + 400-year haenyeo tradition) very strong appeal for senior travelers. 0.75 verified.

senior_general_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.55: Same logic — 4/5 stops accessible to general senior population (alternative for Seongsan = stay at base for haenyeo demonstration). 0.55 calibrated.

beach_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.85: Hamdeok IS the dedicated beach stop — Top 3 Jeju beach (verified VisitKorea ranking). 0.6 understates this; 0.85 reflects beach as a primary tour theme.

culture_level.reasoning: Slight DOWNGRADE 1.0→0.95: Verified 3 strong cultural anchors (Seongeup 600-year town + Haenyeo 400-year tradition + Seongsan haenyeo demo). Hamdeok is recreational not cultural. 0.95 (vs east-signature's lower) — still very strong.
